group.lead_author: Zhihao Zhu (Guoying Gu / Xiangyang Zhu group)
group.funding: National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC); Shanghai Municipal Science and Technology

candidates.title: A variable stiffness robotic gripper based on parallel beam with vision-based force sensing
candidates.method_summary: A 2-finger gripper with parallel-beam variable-stiffness mechanism whose deformation is monitored by an internal camera (vision-based force sensing). Stiffness is tuned by mechanical preload, and contact force is reconstructed from observed beam deflection — eliminating dedicated force/torque sensors.
limitations: Vision-based force sensing limited by lighting and occlusion
Bandwidth restricted by camera frame rate
Only 2-finger pinch grasps demonstrated

group.affiliation: Purdue University / Beijing Institute of Technology

candidates.title: AnyTeleop: A General Vision-Based Dexterous Robot Arm-Hand Teleoperation System
candidates.method_summary: AnyTeleop is a vision-based teleoperation framework for arbitrary arm-hand combinations. A single RGB camera streams human hand pose; an optimization-based retargeting solver matches keypoints to any robot hand morphology, supporting cross-embodiment teleop without hardware-specific calibration. Validated on 4 robot hands and 6 simulators.
limitations: No haptic/tactile feedback to operator
Relies on accurate hand pose estimation; struggles under occlusion
Optimization latency limits high-frequency dynamic tasks

group.affiliation: UC San Diego / NVIDIA / University of Washington

candidates.title: Code as Policies: Language Model Programs for Embodied Control
candidates.method_summary: Reframes LLM-based robot control as code generation: given a natural-language instruction and a set of perception/control APIs (e.g., get_obj_pos, move_to_pose, place_at), an LLM (Codex / GPT-3.5) writes Python policy code that calls those APIs, including loops, conditionals, recursive function definitions, and arithmetic — yielding 'language model programs' (LMPs). Hierarchical Code-as-Policies allows the LLM to define helper functions on the fly when an API is missing. Few-shot prompting with carefully curated example programs is the only training signal; no robot data is required.
limitations: Authors note: relies on a curated set of perception+control APIs — silent failure if an API returns wrong values.
Authors note: long programs increase brittleness; one syntactic error breaks the whole policy.
Authors note: prompting is brittle; performance varies with example selection.
Reader-side: closed-loop reactivity is limited — programs run open-loop unless the prompt explicitly asks for sensor checks inside a while-loop.
Reader-side: assumes Codex-class code competence; smaller open models perform substantially worse.

candidates.title: DeXtreme: Transfer of Agile In-Hand Manipulation from Simulation to Reality
candidates.method_summary: NVIDIA's Allegro-hand sim-to-real cube-reorientation on Isaac Gym (DextremeADR). Follow-up from OpenAI Rubik line but on commodity research hardware. Exhibits GPU-parallel DR scaling.
limitations: Single-task
Allegro is 4-finger — limited to cube-type objects

candidates.title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
candidates.method_summary: Represents robot visuomotor policy as a conditional denoising diffusion process (DDPM or DDIM) over action sequences. Uses receding-horizon chunks of 8-16 actions, conditioned on recent observations via a 1D CNN or Transformer. Handles multimodal demonstrations gracefully (e.g., left vs right push-T) where MSE-trained behavior cloning collapses to the mean. Released open-source pipeline that became the default BC baseline for humanoid and manipulation papers through 2024-2025.
limitations: Inference cost (multiple denoising steps, ~100 ms)
Chunked actions can diverge under replanning
Requires careful horizon/step tuning

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch11 §11.2 Electric Atlas citation. Primary-source-verified via bostondynamics.com/products/atlas + humanoidspecs.com: 56 DoF ✓, 89 kg ✓, 1.5 m ✓, 2.3 m reach ✓, 50 kg payload ✓, 4 h battery ✓, 85-90% efficiency ✓, sub-10 cm foot placement ✓. All 7 quantitative specs match book §11.2. Cross-chapter consistent with Ch04 §4.5. Institutional author (Boston Dynamics); T1.13 flag applies.

candidates.title: LEAP Hand: Low-Cost, Efficient, and Anthropomorphic Hand for Robot Learning
candidates.method_summary: 16-DoF anthropomorphic robot hand using Dynamixel servos and 3D-printed parts; total cost ~$2,000 vs Allegro ~$15K and Shadow Hand ~$100K. Universal abduction-adduction joint design avoids singularities. Open-sourced CAD/firmware made it the de-facto cheap research hand for imitation learning and tactile experiments.
limitations: Dynamixel actuators less back-drivable than tendon designs
No integrated tactile sensing in baseline design
Servo heat under continuous high-load tasks

candidates.title: Learning Fine-Grained Bimanual Manipulation with Low-Cost Hardware (ACT)

aliases.title: Learning fine-grained bimanual manipulation with low-cost hardware (ACT)

candidates.method_summary: ALOHA + Action Chunking Transformer (ACT): low-cost teleop bimanual platform + Transformer policy that predicts action chunks (size 10-50) rather than single actions, reducing compounding error. Default BC baseline for bimanual tasks and used as a comparison model in most humanoid VLA papers since 2024.
limitations: Fixed-base bimanual — no locomotion
Demo-hungry on truly new tasks
Action chunks brittle when sensory context shifts fast

candidates.title: Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models
authors: Open X-Embodiment Collaboration (300+ co-authors across 34 institutions)
candidates.method_summary: Large-scale cross-embodiment dataset: 22 robots, 527 skills, 1M+ trajectories from 34 academic labs. Trains RT-X policies that transfer across embodiments without per-robot engineering. Foundation of OpenVLA training data and the cross-embodiment benchmark used by subsequent VLAs.
limitations: Heterogeneous action spaces require normalization
Humanoid embodiments still underrepresented
Data collection still human-teleop bottlenecked

candidates.title: Orbit: A Unified Simulation Framework for Interactive Robot Learning Environments
candidates.method_summary: Unified simulation framework on top of NVIDIA Isaac Sim integrating RL, IL, motion planning, and teleop with a single GPU-tensorized API. Predecessor/kernel of what became Isaac Lab — introduces the environment manager, reward composer, and cross-sim asset pipeline that Isaac Lab formalized.
limitations: Early-stage API (pre-Isaac Lab refactor)
Scene graph heavy — slower than Isaac Gym headless
Required Omniverse install

group.affiliation: NVIDIA + ETH Zurich RSL + U Toronto (Garg)

candidates.title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
candidates.method_summary: PaLM-E injects continuous sensor observations (RGB images via ViT, object-centric scene representations, robot proprioception) directly into the embedding space of a frozen-or-finetuned PaLM language model by training a small projection from each modality to the LM token space. The result is a single decoder-only transformer (largest variant: PaLM-562B = PaLM-540B + ViT-22B) that handles VQA, image captioning, and robot task planning end-to-end, generating text outputs that include skill names or robot plans. Training is a mixture of internet vision-language data and three robot embodiments: TAMP block manipulation, Language-Table tabletop, and a real Everyday Robots mobile manipulator.
limitations: Authors note: does not produce low-level continuous actions — it outputs high-level skill names that a separate low-level policy must execute.
Authors note: catastrophic forgetting if VL pretraining data is removed; co-training is essential.
Authors note: 562B parameters → infeasible to deploy onboard; inference latency is prohibitive for real-time control.
Reader-side: closed-source model + closed Everyday Robots data → not reproducible.
Reader-side: planning horizon limited by single-prompt context; no explicit memory mechanism.

group.affiliation: Google Research (Robotics at Google + Brain) + TU Berlin

candidates.title: REFLECT: Summarizing Robot Experiences for Failure Explanation and Correction
candidates.method_summary: REFLECT proposes a hierarchical multi-modal robot-experience summarizer that converts raw sensor logs (RGB-D, joint states, force/torque, executed actions) into a textual 'experience summary' over three levels: (1) sensory — caption each frame with a VLM, detect contact/force events; (2) event — segment the trajectory into sub-tasks; (3) task — summarize the whole episode. When a failure occurs, an LLM (GPT-4) is given the experience summary and asked to (a) explain the failure cause, (b) propose a correction plan. The corrected plan is fed back into a planner (e.g., LLM+PDDL) for re-execution. The authors release RoboFail, a benchmark of 100 failure cases across 10 tasks (cooking, cleaning, sorting) for evaluating LLM-based failure analysis.
limitations: Authors note: the hierarchical summary itself can drop information needed to diagnose subtle failures (e.g., misperceived object pose).
Authors note: depends on VLM caption quality; mis-captioning cascades into wrong explanations.
Authors note: most evaluation is in simulation; real-world failure modes are richer.
Reader-side: GPT-4 in the loop → not real-time; correction takes seconds-to-minutes per failure.
Reader-side: assumes failure has already occurred and been detected — no proactive failure prediction.

candidates.title: Review on Human-like Robot Manipulation Using Dexterous Hands
candidates.method_summary: Survey article cataloging recent dexterous-hand manipulation systems (2018-2022) across hardware (Allegro, Shadow, Schunk), control (impedance, MPC, RL), and learning (imitation, RL, sim-to-real). Provides taxonomy tables but limited depth on tactile sensing specifically.
limitations: Tactile sensing receives limited dedicated treatment
Cutoff before 2023's tactile-VLA wave (TacGS, T3, etc.)
Mid-tier journal; less rigorous than IJRR/T-RO surveys

candidates.title: Rotating without Seeing: Towards In-hand Dexterity through Touch
candidates.method_summary: Trains an in-hand object rotation policy using only proprioception and tactile signals (no vision) on an Allegro hand with 16 binary tactile pads. PPO in IsaacGym with massive domain randomization, then zero-shot transferred. Key result: touch-only policy achieves rotation rates competitive with vision-based baselines on diverse objects in the dark.
limitations: Binary tactile pads provide only contact/no-contact; no force gradient
Single-axis rotation task; not full reorientation to target pose
Sim-to-real gap mitigated only by extensive randomization

candidates.title: RT-1: Robotics Transformer for Real-World Control at Scale
candidates.method_summary: RT-1 is a 35M-parameter transformer that maps RGB images and natural-language instructions to discretized robot actions. Trained on a 17-month, ~130k-episode dataset covering 700+ tasks across 13 EDR robots. Demonstrates strong scaling: more data and tasks improve generalization to new objects, scenes, and skills.
limitations: Parallel-jaw only — no dexterous hands or tactile
Action discretization limits fine-precision
Closed-source data and model

group.lead_author: Anthony Brohan (Karol Hausman & Sergey Levine cluster)

candidates.title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
candidates.method_summary: Google DeepMind RT-2: co-trains a PaLI-X VLM on robot action data + web image-text, emitting actions as text tokens. Shows positive transfer from web semantics — e.g., 'move object to extinct animal' invokes dinosaur-adjacent object via language reasoning. Discretizes 6-DoF end-effector deltas into text tokens.
limitations: Closed model
Discrete tokens limit continuous control
Large latency for closed-loop

candidates.title: SayPlan: Grounding Large Language Models using 3D Scene Graphs for Scalable Robot Task Planning
candidates.method_summary: SayPlan grounds LLM planners in pre-built 3D Scene Graphs (3DSG) — hierarchical graphs whose nodes are buildings/floors/rooms/objects with affordance attributes — to enable long-horizon, large-scale (multi-floor) planning. Three innovations: (1) semantic search via collapsible scene-graph API where GPT-4 explores only the subgraph relevant to the instruction (rather than dumping the whole graph in-context), (2) iterative replanning with a classical path planner (Dijkstra) that grounds navigation feasibility into the LLM loop, and (3) a verification step where the LLM checks its own plan against the graph before issuing actions. The system handles instructions like 'find a place to leave a glass of milk that won't spoil' across 28-room office buildings.
limitations: Authors note: assumes a pre-built, accurate 3D scene graph — no online graph construction or correction.
Authors note: replanning loop adds latency; multiple GPT-4 calls per instruction.
Authors note: dynamic scenes (objects move) are not explicitly handled.
Reader-side: depends on a separate 3DSG construction pipeline (e.g., Hydra, Concept Graphs) — not end-to-end.
Reader-side: skill primitives (pick, place, open) are still hand-defined; SayPlan only chooses the sequence.

group.affiliation: Queensland University of Technology (QUT) — Australian Centre for Robotic Vision
group.funding: Australian Research Council; QUT Centre for Robotics

candidates.canonical_id: title:tactile based object orientation detection for blind manipulation
candidates.title: Tactile-based object orientation detection for blind manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Demonstrates tactile-only object orientation estimation for blind in-hand manipulation; uses a small CNN/MLP head on multi-fingered tactile arrays to regress object rotation. Shows that feedback-driven orientation correction is feasible without any visual input. Identifying details (venue, exact authors) limited in the source bibliography.
limitations: Bibliography entry incomplete; venue and exact methodology not verified
Likely small object set; generalization unclear
Tactile-only setup ignores complementary proprioception/vision

group.affiliation: Unknown (Lach et al., to be verified)

candidates.title: Touch and Go: Learning from Human-Collected Vision and Touch
candidates.method_summary: Releases a large in-the-wild visuo-tactile dataset collected by humans pressing a handheld GelSight sensor on diverse outdoor/indoor surfaces while a chest camera captures aligned RGB. ~13k pairs across 20 material categories. Demonstrates self-supervised cross-modal pretraining yields tactile features that transfer to material classification and texture synthesis.
limitations: Static touch only (press-and-release); no dynamic exploration
Single GelSight; no multi-finger or robot data
Domain gap between handheld and robot-mounted GelSight

candidates.title: Xiaomi CyberOne 휴머노이드 플랫폼

candidates.canonical_id: title:brl pisa iit softhand a low cost 3d printed underactuated tendon driven hand with soft and adaptive synergies
candidates.title: BRL/Pisa/IIT SoftHand: A low-cost, 3D-printed, underactuated, tendon-driven hand with soft and adaptive synergies
candidates.method_summary: BRL-led re-implementation of the Pisa/IIT SoftHand using 3D-printed parts and a single tendon-driven actuator with adaptive synergies. Aims to make the soft-hand paradigm replicable in a typical research lab by lowering BOM and assembly complexity. Bibliography entry incomplete in the source list.
limitations: Bibliography entry incomplete; venue and exact year unverified
Underactuated design limits in-hand manipulation dexterity
No integrated tactile sensing in baseline

candidates.title: Do As I Can, Not As I Say: Grounding Language in Robotic Affordances
candidates.method_summary: SayCan grounds LLM-based task planning in robotic affordances by combining two scores per candidate skill: (a) the LLM's probability that the skill is a useful next step given the natural-language instruction (the 'Say' score, computed as token likelihood of the skill description under PaLM-540B/FLAN), and (b) a learned per-skill value/affordance function (the 'Can' score) trained via TD-based RL plus behavior cloning that estimates the probability that the skill will succeed in the current visual state. The product of the two scores selects the next primitive at each step; execution iterates until the LLM proposes a 'done' token. The skill library is ~551 manipulation+navigation primitives executed on a mobile manipulator (Everyday Robots).
limitations: Authors note: skill library is closed and hand-engineered; system cannot invent new primitives.
Authors note: affordance value function must be trained per skill, limiting scalability.
Authors note: LLM has no real-time perceptual feedback during a skill — replanning happens only at skill boundaries.
Reader-side: 'Can' scores can be miscalibrated, leading to safe-but-wrong skill selection.
Reader-side: requires Everyday Robots hardware + extensive teleop data; not reproducible by typical labs.

group.affiliation: Google Research / Everyday Robots (Robotics at Google)
group.lead_author: Michael Ahn (co-led with Anthony Brohan, Brian Ichter, Karol Hausman)

candidates.title: Ego4D: Around the World in 3,000 Hours of Egocentric Video
candidates.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2022
candidates.method_summary: Ego4D is a massive egocentric video dataset: ~3,670 hours from 931 participants in 9 countries, capturing daily activities with head-mounted cameras. Bundled with 5 benchmark suites (episodic memory, hands-and-objects, audio-visual diarization, social interaction, forecasting) — becomes the foundation dataset for hand-object understanding in vision and robotics.
limitations: Camera-only — no tactile or proprioceptive data
Hand pose annotations limited to subset (Hands-and-Objects subset)
Strong camera-mount and demographic biases

candidates.title: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling
candidates.method_summary: Flow Matching as a simulation-free training objective for Continuous Normalizing Flows: regress a velocity field that transports noise to data along simple conditional paths. Foundation for π0's 50 Hz flow-matching action head.
limitations: Sensitive to choice of conditional path
Need for ODE solver at inference

candidates.title: Language Models as Zero-Shot Planners: Extracting Actionable Knowledge for Embodied Agents
candidates.method_summary: Demonstrates that large pretrained language models (GPT-3 175B, Codex) can decompose high-level natural-language tasks (e.g., 'make breakfast') into sequences of mid-level action steps for embodied agents in a zero-shot manner, without any task-specific finetuning. Naive autoregressive sampling produces actions that are semantically reasonable but not executable in a target environment, so the authors introduce a translation step that, at each generation, searches for the most semantically similar admissible action in the environment using a Sentence-BERT cosine-similarity over the agent's allowed action set. They additionally use a smaller LM (GPT-2) to score and re-rank generated steps and use the chosen step as causal context for the next prediction. Evaluated on the VirtualHome household-task benchmark with human-written ground-truth programs.
limitations: Authors note that LMs hallucinate steps that are semantically correct but not executable, motivating the translation module — translation in turn constrains creativity.
Authors note no closed-loop sensory feedback: plans are open-loop and not regrounded as the world changes.
Evaluated only in VirtualHome simulation with discrete admissible actions; no real-robot deployment, no perception module.
Reader-side: action set must be enumerable and embeddable, which does not scale to continuous robot control or partially observable scenes.
Reader-side: plans assume a static, fully-observable household; no recovery from execution failure.

group.funding: Google internal compute; UC Berkeley/CMU PhD funding

candidates.title: Mastering Visual Continuous Control: Improved Data-Augmented Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: DrQ-v2: data-augmentation for pixel-based continuous control. Cited as a vision-RL baseline in humanoid papers that feed images directly. Still used as comparison by pixel-based humanoid policies in 2024-2025.
limitations: Pixel simulation only
Not humanoid-scale directly

candidates.title: ObjectFolder 2.0: A Multisensory Object Dataset for Sim2Real Transfer
candidates.method_summary: Releases 1,000 simulated objects with implicit neural representations of their visual, auditory, and tactile signatures. ObjectFiles render view-conditioned vision, contact-conditioned tactile (TACTO-style), and impact-conditioned audio. Sim-to-real benchmarks on instance recognition, 3D shape reconstruction, and contact localization.
limitations: Tactile signatures rendered, not measured per real object
Object set biased toward household items
Implicit representations costly to query at scale

candidates.title: Sim-to-Real Learning of All Common Bipedal Gaits via Periodic Reward Composition
candidates.method_summary: Demonstrates that periodic-reward composition (hopping + walking + running + skipping) trained in MuJoCo transfers zero-shot to Cassie. Single policy family; clock-phase design generalizes across gait types. Influenced the 'reward shaping via biological gait clocks' pattern in later humanoid RL.
limitations: Clock-phase reward engineering still manual
Cassie-specific tuning

candidates.title: TACTO: A Fast, Flexible, and Open-Source Simulator for High-Resolution Vision-Based Tactile Sensors
candidates.method_summary: GPU-accelerated simulator for vision-based tactile sensors (DIGIT, OmniTact, GelSight) built on PyBullet + custom shading. Renders RGB tactile images at hundreds of FPS by approximating gel deformation as depth-map shading; supports multi-finger setups. De-facto standard for tactile sim-to-real research at Meta.
limitations: Approximate shading; no true elastomer mechanics simulation
Doesn't model marker dynamics or shear with full fidelity
Restricted to vision-based tactile family

candidates.title: A Review of Robot Learning for Manipulation: Challenges, Representations, and Algorithms
candidates.method_summary: Comprehensive review of learning-based manipulation: representations, algorithms, task decomposition, transfer. Pre-VLA baseline frequently cited by 2024-2025 humanoid VLA papers. Ch 10 / Ch 15 benchmark.
limitations: Pre-VLA era
Pre-foundation-model era
Does not cover humanoid whole-body

survey_backrefs.chapter_hint: Ch8 Modern Theory, Ch10 VLA, Ch15 Differentiation

candidates.title: Algorithmic Foundations of Dynamic Bipedal Robots with an Emphasis on Underactuated Locomotion
candidates.venue: Annual Review of Control, Robotics, and Autonomous Systems
candidates.method_summary: Comprehensive survey of HZD, underactuated biped control, and the classical-to-modern bridge. Serves as a reference for Ch 2 foundations and the transition to learning.
limitations: Pre-RL-boom — limited learning coverage

candidates.title: Blind Bipedal Stair Traversal via Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: First fully-learned bipedal RL policy deployed zero-shot on a commercial humanoid (Cassie). Uses LSTM policy over proprioceptive history, trained in MuJoCo with domain randomization over terrain slope, stair dimensions, mass, and actuator gains. Rewards encode periodic gait clock signals inspired by Hybrid Zero Dynamics. Deployed blind (no cameras) on real stairs in academic buildings.
limitations: Blind (no perception)
Stair height assumed within 10-20 cm range
Cassie leaf-spring compliance at shin/tarsus (see hurst2019cassie for q5/q6 mechanical detail) — harder to extend to articulated-ankle humanoids

candidates.title: Convergence Analysis of Soft-Actor-Critic + Entropy Bonus in Continuous Control
candidates.venue: Survey chapter — deep RL for continuous control
candidates.method_summary: Berkeley deep-RL lecture series (Levine) providing didactic framing of PPO vs SAC vs TD3 trade-offs and entropy-bonus explanation. Useful cross-reference in Ch 8 theory primer; cite as pedagogical source rather than primary research.
limitations: Tutorial, not peer-reviewed primary paper
Concepts summarized, specific claims need primary cite

candidates.title: GelSight Wedge: Measuring High-Resolution 3D Contact Geometry with a Compact Robot Finger
candidates.method_summary: Wedge-shaped GelSight variant designed as a slim parallel-jaw fingerpad; uses a tilted gel face plus mirrored optics to fit camera into a finger-sized housing. Recovers high-res 3D depth and shear at ~25µm resolution. Enables tactile feedback in standard parallel grippers without bulky add-ons.
limitations: Wedge geometry biases contact area to one face
Camera framerate (~30-90Hz) limits high-speed manipulation
Gel still requires periodic replacement

candidates.title: Integrated Linkage-Driven Dexterous Anthropomorphic Robotic Hand
candidates.method_summary: Introduces ILDA Hand, a 15-DoF anthropomorphic hand using compact linkage transmissions instead of tendons or direct drive, achieving human-like fingertip force (34N) at human-hand mass (1.1kg). Each finger integrates a 6-axis force/torque sensor at the fingertip. Korean research effort that influenced the SNU tactile-hand line.
limitations: Linkage transmission limits backdrivability vs tendon designs
No skin-level tactile sensing (only fingertip 6-axis FT)
Complex assembly limits open-source replication

group.funding: Korea Institute of Machinery & Materials, NRF Korea

candidates.title: Isaac Gym: High Performance GPU-Based Physics Simulation For Robot Learning
candidates.method_summary: NVIDIA Isaac Gym co-locates rigid-body physics (PhysX) and neural-network policy training on a single GPU. Thousands of parallel environments run in GPU memory; observations and rewards are kept as CUDA tensors directly consumed by PyTorch policies. Eliminates the CPU/GPU tensor copy bottleneck that limited prior RL simulators. Reports 2-3 orders of magnitude throughput improvement vs MuJoCo + CPU.
limitations: PhysX rigid-body only (no fluids/deformables at launch)
Less accurate contact vs MuJoCo MJX in some regimes
Closed-source PhysX core

candidates.title: Learning to Walk in Minutes Using Massively Parallel Deep Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: Uses Isaac Gym to train ANYmal quadruped locomotion with 4096 parallel robot instances on a single workstation. Introduces terrain curriculum (stairs, slopes, gaps) and a PPO-based training recipe that converges in under 20 minutes of wall-clock. Defines the 'legged_gym' task set that became the de facto reference for later humanoid RL.
limitations: Quadruped only in the original paper
Height-map privileged information used for teacher
Reward engineering remains manual

survey_backrefs.chapter_hint: Ch5 GPU Massively Parallel Simulation, Ch6 Learning Canon

candidates.title: ReSkin: Versatile, Replaceable, Lasting Tactile Skins
candidates.method_summary: Magnetic-microparticle elastomer skin that is decoupled from the readout electronics: the skin is a passive, low-cost (<$1) magnetized rubber pad that wears out and is swapped, while the magnetometer board underneath persists. Self-supervised learning calibrates each new skin in minutes.
limitations: Lower spatial resolution than GelSight-class sensors
Magnetic readout sensitive to ferromagnetic environments
Calibration drift across very different skin thicknesses

candidates.title: RMA: Rapid Motor Adaptation for Legged Robots
candidates.method_summary: Two-phase training: Phase 1 trains a base policy conditioned on an environment extrinsics vector (mass, friction, motor strength) given as privileged info. Phase 2 trains an adaptation module that regresses the extrinsics vector from the recent state-action history (last 50 steps) via supervised learning. At deployment, adaptation module runs at 10 Hz while the base policy runs at 100 Hz. Unitree A1 zero-shot on grass, mud, hiking trails without any real-world fine-tuning.
limitations: Relies on teacher extrinsics vector handcrafted per task
Proprioception-only (blind)
Adaptation is amortized SysID, not online optimization

candidates.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
candidates.method_summary: Formalizes diffusion probabilistic models as denoising score matching with Langevin dynamics under a weighted variational bound. Establishes the DDPM training recipe that Diffusion Policy (Chi 2023) and later π0 (flow matching) adapt for robot action generation. Primary reference for Ch 8 generative-model primer.
limitations: Hundreds of denoising steps for sampling
Training cost scales with time-step count

candidates.title: DexPilot: Vision-Based Teleoperation of Dexterous Robotic Hand-Arm System
candidates.method_summary: DexPilot enables glove-free, vision-only teleoperation of a 23-DoF Allegro hand on a 7-DoF arm. Uses depth cameras + neural network to track human hand and retarget motions to robot via constrained optimization, supporting dexterous tasks (cup placement, packaging, ATM card insertion) without specialized capture hardware.
limitations: No tactile/haptic feedback
Multi-camera setup needed for occlusion robustness
Optimization-based retargeting introduces latency

candidates.title: DIGIT: A Novel Design for a Low-Cost Compact High-Resolution Tactile Sensor with Application to In-Hand Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Compact GelSight-style tactile sensor designed for finger-mounted use on multi-fingered hands. Uses 3D-printed shell, OmniVision sensor, three RGB LEDs, and replaceable elastomer gel; total BOM ~$15 at scale. Open-sourced hardware sparked widespread tactile-policy research at Meta AI and beyond.
limitations: Still bulky relative to a human fingerpad; mounting on small fingers difficult
Optical sensor sensitive to gel wear and lighting drift
Requires per-sensor calibration for force estimation

candidates.title: Exploring the role of palm concavity and adaptability in soft synergistic robotic hands
candidates.method_summary: Studies how palm concavity and compliance affect grasp robustness in synergistic soft hands. Designs three palm variants (rigid flat, rigid concave, soft adaptive) for the Pisa/IIT SoftHand and benchmarks them across grasp types from the Feix taxonomy. Shows that adaptive concave palms significantly enlarge the grasp envelope and reduce required precision in pre-grasp positioning.
limitations: Limited to single-hand SoftHand architecture; generalization to other hands unclear
No tactile sensing on the palm surface
Manual object set rather than standardized YCB benchmark

candidates.title: Learning Memory-Based Control for Human-Scale Bipedal Locomotion
candidates.method_summary: Predecessor to Siekmann 2021. LSTM policy for Cassie sim-to-real. Demonstrates periodic gait through clock-based reward design (variant of Hybrid Zero Dynamics). Establishes the LSTM history-encoder paradigm for biped RL.
limitations: Still flat-ground
Clock-phase reward brittle to terrain variation

candidates.title: Learning Quadrupedal Locomotion Over Challenging Terrain
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the teacher-student privileged-information paradigm to legged RL. Teacher is trained with access to terrain height-map and contact ground truth; student is a proprioception-only policy trained via behavioral cloning plus DAgger-style distillation. Student deploys zero-shot on ANYmal across grass, mud, snow, and debris. Uses a temporal convolutional encoder (TCN) over proprioceptive history as an implicit state estimator.
limitations: Blind — no cameras
Student cannot anticipate cliffs/obstacles
Teacher still relies on privileged height-map in sim

candidates.canonical_id: title:openai dactyl solving rubik s cube with a robot hand
candidates.title: OpenAI Dactyl: Solving Rubik's Cube with a robot hand
candidates.method_summary: Trains a Shadow Dexterous Hand to solve a Rubik's Cube via massive-scale sim-to-real RL with Automatic Domain Randomization (ADR). ADR adaptively expands randomization ranges (mass, friction, latency, visual textures) as the policy improves, training on thousands of CPU cores. Achieves real-world cube solving despite the policy never seeing the real robot during training.
limitations: Massive compute: ~$1M+ training cost equivalent
Vision-only state estimation; no tactile use
Specialized to cube task; generalization limited

group.lead_author: OpenAI team (Ilge Akkaya, Marcin Andrychowicz et al.)

candidates.title: Robust Motion In-Betweening (LAFAN1 Dataset)
candidates.method_summary: Releases LAFAN1 mocap dataset (Ubisoft La Forge): 77 minutes of high-quality character animation across 5 actors. Widely used alongside AMASS as a reference-motion source for humanoid RL. Smaller than AMASS but higher per-clip quality and consistency.
limitations: Small vs AMASS
Character animation focus — some motions not physically feasible for humanoids
Requires retargeting for robot embodiments

candidates.title: Tactile innervation densities across the whole body
candidates.method_summary: Compiles whole-body innervation density estimates from histological literature into a unified model. Quantifies that the fingertips host ~240 mechanoreceptors/cm² vs ~10/cm² on the trunk, with the hand containing ~17,000 of the ~230,000 total cutaneous afferents. Provides the biological reference numbers used to motivate hand-focused robotic skin design.
limitations: Aggregates older histological data with varying methods
Does not distinguish receptor types in fine spatial detail
Inter-subject variability not fully quantified

candidates.title: AMASS: Archive of Motion Capture as Surface Shapes
candidates.method_summary: AMASS aggregates 15+ optical mocap datasets into a unified SMPL-H parameterized archive. 40+ hours of motion, 300+ subjects, 11,000+ motions. The de facto reference-motion corpus for humanoid motion-tracking RL (DeepMimic derivatives, OmniRetarget, HumanPlus, HOVER, GR00T N1 data mix).
limitations: Human-body only (no robot retarget — needs OmniRetarget-style post-processing)
Optical mocap biased to lab environments
Limited contact-rich manipulation sequences

survey_backrefs.chapter_hint: Ch6 Learning Canon, Ch8 Modern Theory

candidates.title: Cassie Bipedal Robot and the ATRIAS Lineage
candidates.venue: Agility Robotics / Oregon State (ATRIAS → Cassie → Digit)
candidates.method_summary: Cassie is a research biped built on SLIP (spring-loaded inverted pendulum) principles: centralize mass in torso, minimize leg inertia. Leaf springs are located at shin pitch (q5) and tarsus pitch (q6) joints (the actuated toe is at q7) — NOT at the ankle as shorthand often implies. ATRIAS-descended design. Lineage runs ATRIAS (2014) → Cassie (2017) → Digit (2020, Agility). The passive-dynamics bet distinguishes the Hurst lineage from Kim/MIT's QDD lineage.
limitations: Leaf-spring compliance at shin/tarsus limits some reactive whole-body control
Non-anthropomorphic gait
Dexterous hand integration harder

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch12 §12.2 Figure 02 BMW pilot + OpenAI partnership. Public press (Figure AI blog Aug 2024). Primary-verified.

candidates.title: Learning Agile and Dynamic Motor Skills for Legged Robots
candidates.method_summary: First fully-learned quadruped locomotion that transfers zero-shot to hardware. Key innovation is the actuator network: a neural net trained on real ANYmal motor data (joint state history → joint torque) that replaces the idealized actuator model in simulation, closing a major sim-to-real gap. Uses TRPO with a stochastic policy + randomized dynamics (mass, link length, COM offsets). Recovers from falls and runs at 1.6 m/s, up from 0.8 m/s of the shipped controller.
limitations: Blind (no exteroception)
Actuator network assumes joint-independent dynamics
Only flat terrain demonstrated
Training took 9-11 h on single workstation (pre-GPU-sim era)

candidates.title: Learning Dexterous In-Hand Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Predecessor of Akkaya 2019. Trains a Shadow-Hand policy in sim with DR (cube physics, friction, motor noise) then transfers zero-shot to real hand. First widely-cited proof that deep RL + DR produces sim-to-real dexterous policies.
limitations: Very high compute cost
Tabletop manipulation only
Brittle to contact variations

candidates.title: Learning the signatures of the human grasp using a scalable tactile glove (STAG)
candidates.method_summary: Knitted tactile glove with 548 piezoresistive sensors on the palmar surface; readout via row-column matrix scanning. Trained CNNs on ~135,000 frames of human grasps to identify objects (76% top-1 from a single touch) and estimate object weight. Established the feasibility of dense, low-cost wearable tactile capture.
limitations: Coverage limited to palmar surface (no dorsal or finger-side)
Piezoresistive cells suffer hysteresis and drift
No force calibration; relative pressure only

candidates.title: Mini Cheetah: A Platform for Pushing the Limits of Dynamic Quadruped Control
candidates.method_summary: Presents the Mini Cheetah: 9 kg quadruped with 12 modular QDD actuators, each ~17 Nm peak torque at ~250 Hz control bandwidth. Uses MPC with simplified centroidal dynamics for gait generation. Demonstrates a sim-trained or hand-tuned backflip policy — the first quadruped aerial flip at this size. Establishes the modular low-cost QDD platform template later mirrored by Unitree A1/Go1/Go2.
limitations: Limited payload (~2 kg)
Small battery runtime (~15 min)
No onboard perception

candidates.title: Solving Rubik's Cube with a Robot Hand
candidates.method_summary: Introduces Automatic Domain Randomization (ADR) on top of OpenAI's Shadow-Hand-in-sim platform to solve a Rubik's Cube one-handed. ADR expands distribution ranges until the agent's performance degrades, then repeats — an autonomous curriculum. Landmark sim-to-real demonstration for dexterous manipulation that directly influenced humanoid DR practice.
limitations: Huge compute budget (13k yr CPU equiv)
Task is table-top only
Face-rotation-limited — not whole body

candidates.title: Trends and Challenges in Robot Manipulation

aliases.title: Trends and challenges

candidates.method_summary: Canonical Science-magazine perspective on robot manipulation challenges. Frames the argument that manipulation is fundamentally harder than locomotion due to contact uncertainty, sensing requirements, and task variability. Provides Ch 15 'why manipulation is the real differentiator' baseline.
limitations: Pre-VLA era — does not anticipate foundation-model scaling
High-level framing, not comprehensive survey

candidates.title: Addressing Function Approximation Error in Actor-Critic Methods

aliases.title: Addressing function approximation error in actor-critic methods

candidates.method_summary: TD3 (Twin Delayed DDPG): addresses Q-value overestimation in DDPG via clipped double-Q learning, delayed policy updates, and target-policy smoothing. Workhorse off-policy algorithm for continuous control; used as the backbone of FastTD3 (Seo 2025) which reintroduced off-policy RL to humanoid control.
limitations: Hyperparameter-sensitive
Exploration relies on target-policy noise
Continuous action only

candidates.title: DeepMimic: Example-Guided Deep Reinforcement Learning of Physics-Based Character Skills
candidates.method_summary: Tracks mocap clips with RL: reward is weighted sum of pose, root, velocity, and contact-force terms; phase variable drives the reference clock. Produces physically-plausible character animation with a single policy covering backflip, cartwheel, running. Template for humanoid motion-tracking RL later adopted (OmniH2O, OmniRetarget, HumanPlus).
limitations: Requires per-clip phase variable
Pure simulation — no real-robot transfer in original
Reward weighting hand-tuned

survey_backrefs.chapter_hint: Ch6 Learning Canon, Ch8 Modern Theory Primer

candidates.title: Dynamic Locomotion in the MIT Cheetah 3 through Convex Model-Predictive Control
candidates.method_summary: Convex MPC formulation for MIT Cheetah 3 quadruped. Uses centroidal-dynamics linearization to solve whole-body MPC at ~30 Hz with a 1 kHz lower-level joint tracking. Exemplar of pre-RL MPC-based legged control; widely imitated through 2020 and a reference baseline for Boston Dynamics' hybrid MPC+RL posture.
limitations: Centroidal linearization limits large-excursion dynamics
Quadruped (not humanoid)
Pre-RL — manual gait scheduling

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch11 §11.6 Hyundai acquisition citation. Primary-source-verified (hyundai.com + bostondynamics.com + PRNewswire): $1.1B valuation ✓, 80% Hyundai / 20% SoftBank ✓, June 21, 2021 closing ✓, Hyundai Mobis co-financed portion ✓.

candidates.title: Soft Actor-Critic: Off-Policy Maximum Entropy Deep Reinforcement Learning with a Stochastic Actor
candidates.method_summary: SAC: off-policy actor-critic under the maximum entropy RL framework. Stochastic actor encourages exploration via entropy bonus; twin critics mitigate over-estimation. The default off-policy baseline for robot learning before 2021 and a key building block for FastTD3-style humanoid RL in 2025.
limitations: Requires critic target tuning
Entropy coefficient sensitive unless auto-tuned (addressed in follow-up)

candidates.title: Toward dexterous manipulation with augmented adaptive synergies: The Pisa/IIT SoftHand 2
candidates.method_summary: Pisa/IIT SoftHand 2 implements augmented adaptive synergies: a 2-motor, 19-DoF anthropomorphic hand using soft tendon-driven mechanism with two synergistic actuators encoding human-inspired postural primitives. Adds a second synergy for in-hand manipulation atop the original SoftHand single-synergy design, enabling both robust grasping and dexterous repositioning with minimal control inputs.
limitations: Only 2 actuators limits independent finger control for complex precision tasks
No integrated tactile sensing on the soft fingers in this version
Tendon-based compliance complicates accurate proprioceptive state estimation

group.affiliation: University of Pisa / IIT (Italian Institute of Technology)

candidates.title: AMBIDEX: Cable-Driven Dual-Arm Manipulator from NAVER LABS
candidates.venue: NAVER LABS product / Korea Tech research
candidates.method_summary: Cable-driven compliant dual-arm manipulator built through NAVER LABS + Korea Tech collaboration. Emphasizes human-safe force transfer, low inertia (cable relocates motors to torso), high compliance. Contrasts with QDD-first trend — a Korean alternative design philosophy focused on compliance over backdrivability-via-low-gear. Updated 2024 under 'Software-Defined (SDx) Robotics Innovation' program.
limitations: Cable mechanisms harder to maintain than direct drive
Limited dynamic bandwidth vs QDD
Primarily manipulation — not bipedal

candidates.title: Attention Is All You Need
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the Transformer architecture based entirely on self-attention, dispensing with recurrence. Enables parallel sequence modeling and the history-encoder architecture that replaced LSTMs in later legged RL (Radosavovic 2024, Helix, GR00T). Foundational for Ch 8 and Ch 9 3-Layer architecture.
limitations: Quadratic attention complexity limits very long contexts
Original design is NLP-only

candidates.title: Design of a variable stiffness soft dexterous gripper
candidates.method_summary: Designs a soft 3-finger gripper combining pneumatic muscle actuators with granular jamming for variable stiffness. Each finger can independently switch between compliant (object-conforming) and stiff (load-bearing) modes via vacuum pressure on jamming chambers, giving dexterity-grade adaptability with industrial-grade payload.
limitations: Pneumatic infrastructure limits portability
Slow stiffness transitions (seconds-scale)
No integrated tactile sensing

group.affiliation: University of Salford (UK) — Autonomous Systems and Robotics Research Centre
group.funding: Iraqi government scholarship + UK research funds

candidates.title: Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep Neural Networks from Simulation to the Real World
candidates.method_summary: Seminal paper formalizing Domain Randomization: train on a wide variance of synthetic textures/lighting/dynamics so that real-world becomes 'just another variation'. Demonstrated object localization transferring zero-shot from simulation to real cameras. Foundational reference for every subsequent sim-to-real humanoid pipeline.
limitations: Only perception, not control
Randomization ranges hand-tuned
Predates GPU-parallel dynamics DR

candidates.title: GelSight: High-Resolution Robot Tactile Sensors for Estimating Geometry and Force
candidates.method_summary: Introduces GelSight as a vision-based tactile sensor using a deformable elastomer with painted markers, imaged by an internal camera with controlled LED illumination. Photometric stereo recovers 3D contact surface geometry at ~30µm spatial resolution; marker displacement gives shear/normal force fields. Established the dominant paradigm for high-resolution tactile sensing.
limitations: Bulky form factor (camera + optics) limits hand integration
Frame-rate limited by camera (~30-60Hz)
Gel wear and re-coating required after extended use

candidates.canonical_id: title:impact mitigation factor imf and mit cost of bandwidth per ampere cba for legged design
candidates.title: Impact Mitigation Factor (IMF) and MIT Cost-of-Bandwidth-per-Ampere (CBA) for legged design
MANO: A Hand Model with Articulated and Non-rigid Deformations

aliases.title: Embodied hands (MANO)

candidates.method_summary: MANO is a learned, low-dimensional parametric hand model derived from ~1000 high-resolution 3D scans of hands in many poses. Combines articulated kinematics (16 joints, 51 pose parameters) with PCA-based shape (10 components) and pose-dependent corrective blendshapes for realistic non-rigid skin deformation. Becomes the de facto standard hand representation across vision, graphics, and robot retargeting.
limitations: Skin-only model — no internal bone/tendon dynamics
Limited demographic diversity in scan dataset
No tactile or contact priors

group.affiliation: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems (MPI-IS)

candidates.title: Proprioceptive Actuator Design in the MIT Cheetah: Impact Mitigation and High-Bandwidth Physical Interaction for Dynamic Legged Robots
candidates.method_summary: Establishes design principles for proprioceptive actuators: high gap-radius custom outer-rotor BLDC with low gear ratio (5.8:1 single-stage planetary). Eliminates load-side torque sensors; instead uses motor current as a torque proxy for high-bandwidth (>100 Hz) force control. Defines the Impact Mitigation Factor (IMF) as a normalized metric for backdrivability at impact. Validates on MIT Cheetah 2 with peak foot forces >450 N at contact times of 85 ms.
limitations: Torque estimates require thermal/current calibration
Limited output torque vs. high-reduction systems
Heat dissipation limits continuous duty at high load

candidates.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
candidates.method_summary: Proposes PPO, a policy-gradient method that optimizes a clipped surrogate objective approximating the TRPO trust region while staying easy to implement with first-order SGD. Default choice for modern legged-RL pipelines (legged_gym, Isaac Lab, Humanoid-Gym) due to stability under heavy domain randomization and parallelism.
limitations: On-policy — sample-inefficient vs SAC/TD3 when real-world data is costly
Hyperparam sensitive (clip ratio, batch size, learning rate)
Struggles with long-horizon sparse rewards without reward shaping

candidates.title: Shape memory alloy-based soft gripper with variable stiffness for compliant and effective grasping
candidates.method_summary: Develops a soft gripper using shape-memory-alloy (SMA) wires for both actuation and stiffness modulation. Joule heating activates SMA fingers to bend, while a second SMA layer changes stiffness, enabling a single-material approach to compliant grasping with electrical control rather than pneumatics.
limitations: Slow thermal response of SMA
Heat dissipation limits cycle frequency
Power consumption high relative to grasp force

candidates.title: A novel, variable stiffness robotic gripper based on integrated soft actuating and particle jamming
candidates.method_summary: Integrates pneumatic soft actuation with particle jamming inside the same finger module. Inflation drives flexion while jamming under vacuum locks the finger shape, decoupling motion and stiffening so the gripper can wrap around objects then stiffen to hold heavy loads.
limitations: Requires both compressed air and vacuum supply
Granular media wear over many cycles
Slow jamming transition (~1-2 s)

group.affiliation: University of Hong Kong (Yonghua Chen group)

candidates.canonical_id: title:underactuated modular finger with pull in mechanism for a robotic gripper
candidates.title: Underactuated modular finger with pull-in mechanism for a robotic gripper
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a modular underactuated robotic finger with a pull-in mechanism that wraps around objects upon contact. Single-tendon actuation drives multi-joint flexion through a passive linkage that adapts to object geometry, enabling robust enveloping grasps across diverse shapes with only one motor per finger.
limitations: Open-loop without tactile or force sensing
Limited fingertip dexterity for precision pinch tasks
Bench evaluation only, no integrated arm-hand experiments

candidates.title: A Whole-Body Model Predictive Control Framework for Humanoid Robots
candidates.method_summary: 27-DoF HRP-2 whole-body MPC using differential dynamic programming (DDP, predecessor of later TROPIC / Crocoddyl) over a 20 ms trajectory time step, running at ~20 Hz MPC fast-time on an offboard 12-core desktop and feeding a 200 Hz onboard PD position-control loop. Representative of late-orthodox-stack effort to replace LIPM with full-dynamics MPC; relevant context for Ch 1-2-11 MPC discussion.
limitations: Brittle to model mismatch
Computationally heavy
Pre-learning era

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch11 §11.2 hydraulic Atlas specs citation. 1.88 m / 158 kg / 28 DoF all standard DRC-era canonical. Community-verified.

candidates.canonical_id: title:a compliant underactuated hand for robust manipulation
candidates.title: A compliant, underactuated hand for robust manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the iHY (iRobot-Harvard-Yale) hand: a 3-finger, 5-actuator compliant underactuated gripper using flexure joints and tendons. Demonstrates that mechanical compliance and underactuation, combined with simple control, achieve robust grasping and basic in-hand manipulation under sensor-poor and uncertain conditions, validated on hundreds of grasp trials.
limitations: Only 5 DoF — limited fine manipulation
Tendon stretch and compliance reduce position accuracy
Tactile sensing limited to fingertip pressure pads

candidates.title: Adaptive Synergies for the Design and Control of the Pisa/IIT SoftHand
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the Pisa/IIT SoftHand: a 19-DoF, single-motor underactuated anthropomorphic hand exploiting a postural synergy + soft-rolling-contact joints. The 'first synergy' (Santello et al. 1998) is implemented mechanically via tendon routing, allowing one motor to drive a continuum of human-like grasps that adapt to object shape via mechanical compliance.
limitations: Single synergy limits in-hand manipulation primitives
No fingertip tactile sensing in original design
Tendon wear under heavy use

candidates.title: Design and Control Considerations for High-Performance Series Elastic Actuators
candidates.method_summary: Series-elastic actuator (SEA) design + control: elastic element in series with motor, load-side deflection sensor → direct torque estimation. The primary pre-QDD approach to compliant legged actuation. Used in Cassie/Digit (Agility), ATRIAS, and early NASA Valkyrie. Useful Ch 4 contrast baseline for QDD vs SEA philosophies.
limitations: Lower bandwidth than QDD
Spring adds inertia and tuning overhead
Harder to model for RL sim-to-real

candidates.title: Introduction to Humanoid Robotics
candidates.method_summary: Canonical textbook summarizing the LIPM / ZMP / preview-control / whole-body QP lineage. Provides the formal derivations that underlie the orthodox humanoid stack covered in Ch 1-2. Primary reference for pre-RL humanoid kinematics, dynamics, and control.
limitations: Does not cover post-2015 RL-based control
Pre-QDD actuator analysis

candidates.title: Optimization-based Full Body Control for the DARPA Robotics Challenge
candidates.method_summary: Documents CMU's whole-body QP controller for the hydraulic Atlas at the DARPA Robotics Challenge. Decomposes control as high-level footstep/manipulation planners producing task-space trajectories, then a 1 kHz full-body inverse dynamics QP that computes joint torques subject to friction cone, joint limit, and centroidal dynamics constraints. Reports qualitative performance on DRC tasks (door, valve, drill) plus the brittleness under unmodeled contact events.
limitations: Brittle under contact surprises
Requires accurate dynamic and frictional models
High engineering burden per scenario
Planning/control decoupling fails under large disturbances

candidates.title: Control of Legged Robots with Optimal Distribution of Contact Forces
candidates.method_summary: Whole-body QP for legged robots with optimal contact-force distribution. Formalizes the inverse-dynamics hierarchy that Feng 2014 and Koenemann 2015 later deployed. Key reference for how the orthodox stack handled multi-contact dynamics before RL.
limitations: Requires accurate dynamic + friction models
Brittle under contact surprises
Pre-RL era

candidates.title: Design Principles for Highly Efficient Quadrupeds and Implementation on the MIT Cheetah Robot
candidates.method_summary: MIT Cheetah design-principles paper: custom outer-rotor BLDC + coaxial planetary gear, low-impedance actuation, efficient leg structure. Pre-Wensing-2017 origin of the QDD lineage. Introduces the 'cost of transport' framing as a design figure of merit.
limitations: Early Cheetah — no stable dynamic balance control yet
Pre-RL
Fragile gearing

candidates.title: Reinforcement Learning in Robotics: A Survey
candidates.method_summary: Pre-deep-RL survey of reinforcement-learning techniques in robotics. Useful Ch 2 / Ch 8 bridge showing the state-of-the-art before 2015 and the gap that GPU-parallel sim + deep policies closed.
limitations: Pre-deep-RL
Limited sim-to-real discussion

candidates.title: Capturability-based Analysis and Control of Legged Locomotion, Part 2: Application to M2V2, a Lower-Body Humanoid
candidates.method_summary: M2V2 hardware validation companion to Pratt 2006 Humanoids capture-point paper. Extends capturability formalism with N-step capture regions, then applies the control algorithm to M2V2, a 3D force-controlled lower-body humanoid with 12 actuated leg DoF. Evaluated on push-recovery while single-legged balancing and on walking tasks, both in simulation and on M2V2 hardware. Primary source for the 0.3 m CP-displacement / ~10% body-weight-impulse M2V2 numbers mis-attributed to Pratt 2006.
limitations: Lower-body only (no torso/arms)
Pre-RL era control
12 DoF limits full-humanoid generality

candidates.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
Tactile Sensing: From Humans to Humanoids
candidates.method_summary: Seminal survey bridging biological and engineered tactile sensing for humanoid robots. Catalogs transduction principles (capacitive, piezoresistive, piezoelectric, optical) and identifies key requirements: spatial resolution ~1mm, temporal bandwidth >100Hz, multimodal coverage. Sets the agenda for the next decade of tactile skin development at IIT and beyond.
limitations: Pre-dates GelSight/DIGIT vision-based tactile revolution
Limited coverage of learning-based tactile processing
Hardware focus; software/representation receives less attention

candidates.title: Coding and Use of Tactile Signals from the Fingertips in Object Manipulation Tasks
candidates.method_summary: Foundational neuroscience review on how human fingertip mechanoreceptors (FA-I, SA-I, FA-II, SA-II) encode contact events during manipulation. Identifies discrete tactile events (contact, slip, release) as the key control signals, with sub-100ms reflexive grip-force adjustments via spinal/cortical loops. Establishes the conceptual basis for event-driven tactile control in robotics.
limitations: Biological data not directly transferable to engineered sensors
Focus on fingertip; palm and finger-side innervation less covered
Pre-2010 review predates modern vision-based tactile sensors

candidates.title: Feedback Control of Dynamic Bipedal Robot Locomotion
candidates.method_summary: Canonical text on Hybrid Zero Dynamics (HZD), the formal underpinning of underactuated biped control that influenced Cassie / Digit gait designs and inspired clock-phase reward structures in later Siekmann RL papers.
limitations: Pre-RL
Assumes known model

candidates.title: Capture Point: A Step toward Humanoid Push Recovery
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the capture point (CP) concept: the point on the ground where a biped can step to bring itself to rest, derived from the LIPM orbital energy. Proposes N-step capture regions and uses them for reactive footstep placement in response to pushes. Validated on a Yobotics-sim planar biped with flywheel body. M2V2 hardware validation appears in follow-up Pratt/Koolen 2012 IJRR paper.
limitations: LIPM assumption breaks down under large CoM excursions
Reactive stepping is 1-2 steps only
Real-world noisy state estimation degrades CP

candidates.title: Biped walking pattern generation by using preview control of zero-moment point
candidates.method_summary: Formulates bipedal walking pattern generation as tracking a reference zero-moment-point (ZMP) trajectory with a Linear Inverted Pendulum Model (LIPM) whose center of mass height is held constant. Uses preview control (a discrete-time LQR with a finite preview window of future ZMP references, typically 1.6 s / 1.6× step period) so that the CoM trajectory can anticipate upcoming steps. Demonstrated on HRP-2P with step period around 0.8 s and sinusoidal foot placements.
limitations: Assumes constant CoM height (LIPM)
Requires precise ZMP reference planned offline
Fails under large unmodeled disturbances or foot-slip
No explicit reasoning about contact uncertainty

candidates.title: Hands for Dexterous Manipulation and Robust Grasping: A Difficult Road Toward Simplicity
candidates.venue: IEEE Transactions on Robotics and Automation

aliases.title: Hands for dexterous manipulation

candidates.method_summary: Highly cited essay arguing that the field's earlier obsession with high-DoF anthropomorphic hands (Utah/MIT, Stanford-JPL) was misguided; advocates synergy-based, underactuated, compliant designs that exploit task mechanics. Lays the conceptual groundwork for the Pisa/IIT SoftHand and Schunk SDH families.
limitations: Essay format; no original experiments
Pre-dates modern learning-based controllers that revive high-DoF designs
Italian/European bias in cited robot platforms

candidates.title: Robotic Grasping and Contact: A Review
candidates.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation
candidates.method_summary: Canonical ICRA review of grasp analysis: form/force closure conditions, contact modeling (point, soft-finger, friction cones), grasp matrix and Jacobian formulations, and stability metrics. Standard reference textbook treatment for analytical grasp synthesis algorithms used through the 2010s.
limitations: Pre-data-driven era; no learning-based grasp synthesis
Rigid-body assumption; soft contact treated heuristically
Limited treatment of multi-finger in-hand manipulation

group.affiliation: University of Pisa / University of Pennsylvania

candidates.canonical_id: title:the kinematics of contact and grasp
candidates.title: The Kinematics of Contact and Grasp
candidates.method_summary: Montana's seminal IJRR paper derives the differential kinematics of point contact between two smooth surfaces, expressing contact-point motion (rolling and sliding velocities) in terms of relative motion of the two bodies and the local first/second fundamental forms (curvature) of each surface. The framework yields a closed-form set of ODEs governing how contact coordinates evolve, and provides the geometric foundation for analyzing rolling, sliding, and twisting (with or without spin) at fingertip-object contacts. It is the canonical reference for contact-state evolution in robotic grasping and manipulation theory, underpinning later work on dexterous in-hand manipulation, grasp planning, and rolling-contact control.
limitations: Assumes smooth (C2) surfaces — does not handle edges, vertices, or non-convex contact
Point-contact model — no contact patch, no soft-finger compliance
Frictionless or simple Coulomb friction — no stick-slip transitions
No force or tactile feedback model — purely kinematic
Real fingertips are deformable; soft-contact extensions came decades later

group.affiliation: Harvard University Division of Applied Sciences (at the time of writing)

candidates.title: Impedance Control: An Approach to Manipulation
candidates.venue: Journal of Dynamic Systems, Measurement, and Control

aliases.title: Impedance control

candidates.method_summary: Foundational three-part paper introducing impedance control: instead of regulating position or force separately, control the dynamic relationship (mass-spring-damper) between manipulator and environment. Provides theoretical justification for compliant interaction during contact-rich tasks; underpins virtually all modern force/admittance controllers.
limitations: Theoretical; assumes accurate dynamic models
Linear analysis; nonlinear contact dynamics treated only heuristically
Pre-dates modern data-driven impedance learning
